description_long: This is a vintage corded telephone with a round, wooden base featuring a series of small, dark buttons and two cream-colored panels. The handset is cylindrical, made of wood and metal, and is connected to the base by a long, fabric-covered cord. The earpiece and mouthpiece are both black and have a classic, flared design, attached to the handset by metal fixtures. The base is adorned with metallic accents and a central, raised column that supports the cradle for the handset. The overall design is ornate and reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, with a combination of wood, metal, and bakelite or plastic elements. The cord is visibly flexible and extends from the base, suggesting the phone is meant to rest on a desk or table.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a round wooden base, ornate metal accents, and a cylindrical handset connected by a fabric-covered cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset resting on the cradle, the round wooden base, and the button panel with cream-colored insets.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the cord extending from the base and the back of the handset, with fewer visible details on the base.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the profile of the handset, the raised central column, and the cord looping outward from the base.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view showing the back of the base, the cord, and the side of the handset, with the button panel partially visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
